TYPE_MISMATCH.help: Check that the type annotation matches the inferred expression type.

TYPE_MISMATCH_ASSIGN.help: Ensure the variable's previous binding type matches the current assignment.

RETURN_TYPE_MISMATCH.help: Check that the return statement matches the declared return type.

TYPE_VAR_UNDECLARED.message: Type variable '{name}' is used in '{func}' but not declared in its type parameters.
TYPE_VAR_UNDECLARED.help: Declare used type variables in the function signature's 'of' clause.

TYPE_PARAM_UNUSED.message: Type parameter '{name}' on '{func}' is declared but not used.
TYPE_PARAM_UNUSED.help: Remove unused type parameters to avoid confusion.

TYPEVAR_LIKE_UNDECLARED.message: Type variable-like '{name}' is used in '{func}' but not declared; declare it with 'of {name}'.
TYPEVAR_LIKE_UNDECLARED.help: For names that look like type variables, declare them in the 'of' clause.

TYPEVAR_INCONSISTENT.help: Ensure all usage sites of a type variable produce the same concrete type.

IF_BRANCH_MISMATCH.message: If branch type mismatch: then {thenType} vs else {elseType}
IF_BRANCH_MISMATCH.help: Ensure both branches of an if expression return the same type.

MATCH_BRANCH_MISMATCH.message: Match case return types differ: {expected} vs {actual}
MATCH_BRANCH_MISMATCH.help: Check that all match branches return the same type.

INTEGER_PATTERN_TYPE.message: Integer pattern used on non-Int scrutinee ({scrutineeType})
INTEGER_PATTERN_TYPE.help: Only use integer patterns on Int-typed match expressions.

UNKNOWN_FIELD.help: Check that the field name is correct for the data type.

FIELD_TYPE_MISMATCH.help: Verify the field initializer expression matches the declared type.

MISSING_REQUIRED_FIELD.help: Provide all required fields declared in the data type.

NOT_CALL_ARITY.help: Adjust the not() call to have exactly 1 argument.

AWAIT_TYPE.message: await expects Maybe<T> or Result<T,E>, got {type}
AWAIT_TYPE.help: Only use await on Maybe or Result types.

DUPLICATE_ENUM_CASE.message: Duplicate enum case '{case}' in match on {type}.
DUPLICATE_ENUM_CASE.help: Remove duplicate enum branches to keep the match concise.

NON_EXHAUSTIVE_MAYBE.message: Non-exhaustive match on Maybe type; missing {missing} case.
NON_EXHAUSTIVE_MAYBE.help: Add both null and non-null branches for Maybe matches.

NON_EXHAUSTIVE_ENUM.help: Add all uncovered enum branches, or add a wildcard.

AMBIGUOUS_INTEROP_NUMERIC.message: Ambiguous interop call '{target}': mixing numeric kinds (Int={hasInt}, Long={hasLong}, Double={hasDouble}). Overload resolution may widen/box implicitly.
AMBIGUOUS_INTEROP_NUMERIC.help: Unify numeric argument types in interop calls to avoid implicit boxing and widening.

LIST_ELEMENT_TYPE_MISMATCH.message: List literal element type mismatch: expected {expected}, got {actual}
LIST_ELEMENT_TYPE_MISMATCH.help: Ensure all elements in a list literal have the same type.

OPTIONAL_EXPECTED.message: Optional value required here: expected Maybe or Option, but got {actual}
OPTIONAL_EXPECTED.help: Pass a Maybe/Option type or explicitly wrap the value.

WORKFLOW_COMPENSATE_TYPE.message: Compensate block for step '{step}' must return Result<Unit, {expectedErr}>, got {actual}
WORKFLOW_COMPENSATE_TYPE.help: Ensure the compensate block returns Result<Unit, E> where E is the step error type.

WORKFLOW_COMPENSATE_MISSING.message: Step '{step}' performs side effects but does not define a compensate block.
WORKFLOW_COMPENSATE_MISSING.help: Provide a compensate block for steps with IO side effects to enable rollback.

WORKFLOW_RETRY_INVALID.message: Workflow retry max attempts must be greater than zero (actual: {maxAttempts}).
WORKFLOW_RETRY_INVALID.help: Set retry.maxAttempts to a positive integer.

WORKFLOW_TIMEOUT_INVALID.message: Workflow timeout must be greater than zero milliseconds (actual: {milliseconds}).
WORKFLOW_TIMEOUT_INVALID.help: Set the timeout to a positive value to ensure compensate logic can be triggered.

WORKFLOW_MISSING_IO_EFFECT.message: Workflow '{func}' must declare @io effect before using a 'workflow' block.
WORKFLOW_MISSING_IO_EFFECT.help: Add @io effect declaration to the function header.

WORKFLOW_UNDECLARED_CAPABILITY.message: Workflow '{func}' step '{step}' uses capability {capability} that is not declared on the function header.
WORKFLOW_UNDECLARED_CAPABILITY.help: Declare {capability} in the function header or adjust the step code.

COMPENSATE_NEW_CAPABILITY.message: Compensate block for step '{step}' in function '{func}' introduces new capability {capability} that does not appear in the main step body.
COMPENSATE_NEW_CAPABILITY.help: Compensate blocks can only reuse capabilities from the main step body.

WORKFLOW_UNKNOWN_STEP_DEPENDENCY.message: Workflow step '{step}' depends on undefined step '{dependency}'.
WORKFLOW_UNKNOWN_STEP_DEPENDENCY.help: Only reference declared step names in the current workflow.

WORKFLOW_CIRCULAR_DEPENDENCY.help: Remove or restructure circular dependencies to enable topological execution.

DECIMAL_DOUBLE_MIXING.message: Cannot combine Decimal and Double in '{operator}'. Double is binary floating-point and would inject rounding error into an exact Decimal. Use Decimal literals (e.g. 1.08m) on both sides, or Int/Long (exact promotion).
DECIMAL_DOUBLE_MIXING.help: Make both operands Decimal (suffix m), or use Int/Long which promote exactly to Decimal.

PII_ASSIGN_DOWNGRADE.message: Cannot assign PII data to lower-level target: {source} -> {target}
PII_ASSIGN_DOWNGRADE.help: Use a sanitization function or declare a matching @pii level on the target.

PII_SINK_UNSANITIZED.message: PII level {level} data output to {sinkKind} without sanitization
PII_SINK_UNSANITIZED.help: Call redact() or tokenize() before output to reduce sensitivity.

PII_ARG_VIOLATION.message: PII argument type mismatch: expected {expected}, got {actual}
PII_ARG_VIOLATION.help: Check the function signature to ensure PII levels and categories match.

DUPLICATE_IMPORT_ALIAS.help: Use unique aliases for different imports to avoid shadowing.

UNDEFINED_VARIABLE.help: Declare and initialize the variable before use.

MULTIPLE_ENTRY_RULES.help: Keep at most one Rule annotated with @entry in a module.

IMPORT_SYMBOL_CONFLICT.help: Adjust the import alias or the local top-level declaration name to avoid the import symbol conflict.

DUPLICATE_SYMBOL.message: Symbol '{name}' is already defined in this scope.
DUPLICATE_SYMBOL.help: Choose a different name or check for unintended duplicate declarations.

EFF_MISSING_IO.message: Function '{func}' may perform I/O but is missing @io effect.
EFF_MISSING_IO.help: Declare @io effect for functions that perform I/O.

EFF_MISSING_CPU.message: Function '{func}' may perform CPU-bound work but is missing @cpu (or @io) effect.
EFF_MISSING_CPU.help: Declare @cpu or @io effect for CPU-intensive functions.

EFF_SUPERFLUOUS_IO_CPU_ONLY.message: Function '{func}' declares @io but only CPU-like work found; @io subsumes @cpu and may be unnecessary.
EFF_SUPERFLUOUS_IO_CPU_ONLY.help: If the function only does CPU work, consider removing the redundant @io declaration.

EFF_SUPERFLUOUS_IO.message: Function '{func}' declares @io but no obvious I/O found.
EFF_SUPERFLUOUS_IO.help: Confirm @io is needed; remove if no I/O behavior exists.

EFF_SUPERFLUOUS_CPU.message: Function '{func}' declares @cpu but no obvious CPU-bound work found.
EFF_SUPERFLUOUS_CPU.help: Remove the redundant @cpu declaration or add corresponding CPU work.

EFF_INFER_MISSING_IO.message: Function '{func}' missing @io effect declaration, inference requires IO.
EFF_INFER_MISSING_IO.help: Add @io effect based on inference results.

EFF_INFER_MISSING_CPU.message: Function '{func}' missing @cpu effect declaration, inference requires CPU (or @io).
EFF_INFER_MISSING_CPU.help: Add @cpu or @io effect based on inference results.

EFF_INFER_REDUNDANT_IO.message: Function '{func}' declares @io but no IO side effects inferred.
EFF_INFER_REDUNDANT_IO.help: Confirm whether to keep the @io declaration.

EFF_INFER_REDUNDANT_CPU.message: Function '{func}' declares @cpu but no CPU side effects inferred.
EFF_INFER_REDUNDANT_CPU.help: Remove the @cpu declaration if no CPU side effects exist.

EFF_INFER_REDUNDANT_CPU_WITH_IO.message: Function '{func}' declares both @cpu and @io; @cpu is redundant since @io is required.

EFFECT_VAR_UNDECLARED.help: Add the effect type parameter to the function signature's effect parameter list.

EFFECT_VAR_UNRESOLVED.message: Effect variable {vars} could not be resolved to concrete effects
EFFECT_VAR_UNRESOLVED.help: Provide explicit effects (pure/cpu/io/workflow) or remove unused effect variables.

CAPABILITY_NOT_ALLOWED.message: Function '{func}' requires {cap} capability but manifest for module '{module}' denies it.
CAPABILITY_NOT_ALLOWED.help: Update the capability manifest or modify the function to comply.

EFF_CAP_MISSING.message: Function '{func}' uses {cap} capability but header declares [{declared}].
EFF_CAP_MISSING.help: Declare the actually used capabilities in the function header.

EFF_CAP_SUPERFLUOUS.message: Function '{func}' declares {cap} capability but it is not used.
EFF_CAP_SUPERFLUOUS.help: Remove unused capability declarations for clarity.

CAPABILITY_INFER_MISSING_IO.message: Function '{func}' uses IO capabilities [{capabilities}] but is missing @io effect (e.g., {calls}).
CAPABILITY_INFER_MISSING_IO.help: Declare @io effect in the function header, or remove related calls to stay pure.

CAPABILITY_INFER_MISSING_CPU.message: Function '{func}' performs CPU capability calls (e.g., {calls}) but declares neither @cpu nor @io effect.
CAPABILITY_INFER_MISSING_CPU.help: Add @cpu or @io effect to cover CPU capabilities.

PII_HTTP_UNENCRYPTED.message: PII data transmitted over HTTP without encryption
PII_HTTP_UNENCRYPTED.help: Use an encrypted channel (HTTPS) or sanitize before transmitting PII data.

PII_ANNOTATION_MISSING.message: PII annotation missing for value flowing into '{sink}'
PII_ANNOTATION_MISSING.help: Add @pii annotation to sensitive data for tracking.

PII_SENSITIVITY_MISMATCH.help: Adjust the data sensitivity level or update the process requirements.

PII_MISSING_CONSENT_CHECK.message: Function '{func}' processes PII data without consent check (GDPR Art. 6)
PII_MISSING_CONSENT_CHECK.help: Call checkConsent() or add @consent_required annotation before processing PII data.

PII_ANALYZER_FAILED.message: PII safety analysis failed for this module — the editor cannot verify whether sensitive data is correctly handled. This policy should not be deployed until the analysis succeeds. Internal reason: {reason}
PII_ANALYZER_FAILED.help: Try saving and reloading the file. If the error persists, contact your administrator or report this issue with the source code attached.

ASYNC_START_NOT_WAITED.help: Call wait on started async tasks to ensure completion.

ASYNC_WAIT_NOT_STARTED.message: Waiting for async task '{task}' that was never started
ASYNC_WAIT_NOT_STARTED.help: Ensure the task name in wait matches a started task.

ASYNC_DUPLICATE_START.message: Async task '{task}' started multiple times ({count} occurrences)
ASYNC_DUPLICATE_START.help: Avoid starting the same task multiple times; reuse or rename.

ASYNC_DUPLICATE_WAIT.message: Async task '{task}' waited multiple times ({count} occurrences)
ASYNC_DUPLICATE_WAIT.help: Ensure each task is waited on only once, or use a separate synchronization mechanism.

ASYNC_WAIT_BEFORE_START.message: Wait for async task '{task}' occurs before any matching start
ASYNC_WAIT_BEFORE_START.help: Execute start before wait, and ensure both are on compatible control paths.

PII_IMPLICIT_UPLEVEL.help: Add explicit type annotations for level changes to aid auditing.

PII_SINK_UNKNOWN.message: PII data may flow to {sinkKind} without annotation
PII_SINK_UNKNOWN.help: Add @pii annotation to track sensitive data flow.

WORKFLOW_RETRY_INCONSISTENT.message: Workflow retry configuration may be unreasonable: {reason}
WORKFLOW_RETRY_INCONSISTENT.help: Check the combination of total wait time, maxAttempts, and backoff strategy.

WORKFLOW_TIMEOUT_UNREASONABLE.message: Workflow timeout configuration may be unreasonable: {reason}
WORKFLOW_TIMEOUT_UNREASONABLE.help: Check whether the timeout value is too large or too small.
